---
title: "Linear Models"
documenttype: Handout
author:
  - name: Saaleh Poovathumkadavil
toc: true
abstract: "A minimal regression note for interpreting coefficients, residuals, and model-based prediction."

version: [v.1.0]
date: last-modified
execute:
  echo: fenced
  freeze: auto
bibliography: references.bib
bibliographystyle: "springer-humanities-author-date"
format:
  tufte-inspired-html: default
---


# Model Form

A simple linear model writes the response as a baseline plus a slope times a
predictor:

$$
y_i=\beta_0+\beta_1x_i+\epsilon_i.
$$

Before fitting a model, review the data representation in
[[Vector Basics|computing/r/vector-basics]] and the uncertainty language in
[[Random Variables|statistics/probability/random-variables]].

# Residuals

Residuals are observed errors from the fitted line. Patterns in residuals often
show that the model is missing structure.
